reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the field name 'mmlist' is misleading, as it suggests a list of all mm_structs, and requested that it be renamed to include 'swap'
reviews.raw_body:
The patch is fine, but the name of the field is awful. Seeing mmlist I
assumed it listed all mm_structs. It has to have "swap" somewhere in the
field name.

-- 
  Kiryl Shutsemau / Kirill A. Shutemov

reviews.author: Usama Arif (author)
reviews.summary: Author suggests making a change to use swap_mmlist instead of mmlist, but does not confirm whether this will be done in the current patch or as a separate patch
sentiment_signals: request for feedback on alternative approach
reviews.raw_body:
I can send a separate patch once this is replaced to replace mmlist with
swap_mmlist if that sounds better?

Thanks
Usama

2026-07-06.patch_summary: This patch extracts a helper function, mm_prepare_for_swap_entries(), from the try_to_unmap_one() and copy_nonpresent_pte() functions in the Linux kernel's memory management code. The function adds an mm_struct to the init_mm.mmlist circular list when it is empty, ensuring that swapoff can find and unuse its swap entries. This refactoring allows for reuse of this logic by upcoming PMD-level swap entry code paths.
